steps.action: Launch QEMU with 9p filesystem, keyboard, GPU, RTC
steps.observed: QEMU booted successfully — all services started (init, core, compositor, text-editor, GPU driver, input drivers)
Serial log confirms: 'display pipeline running', 'entering render loop (frame-scheduled)'

steps.action: Send keystrokes h-e-l-l-o via monitor socket with 0.15s delay
steps.observed: All 5 keystrokes sent successfully via nc

steps.action: Wait 1 second for rendering, then capture screendump
steps.observed: PPM file captured (3072016 bytes), converted to PNG (7642 bytes)

steps.action: Visually verify screenshot with Read tool
steps.expected: Title bar 'Text' visible, clock visible, 'hello' text visible, cursor visible, no artifacts
steps.observed: Title bar shows 'Text' (top-left). Clock shows '16:36:34' (top-right). Document area shows 'hello' text below title bar. Thin vertical cursor visible immediately after 'hello'. Dark background is clean with no visual artifacts or corruption.

steps.action: Check serial log for panics or errors
steps.observed: grep for panic/crash/error/fault/abort returned zero matches — clean boot and operation

evidence.consoleErrors: none — grep for panic/crash/error/fault/abort found zero matches in serial output

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-CROSS-001 confirmed: QEMU boots cleanly, title bar ('Text'), clock ('16:36:34'), typed text ('hello'), and cursor all render correctly with no visual artifacts. Serial log shows no panics or errors.
